name: DOD-Prompt-Military-Relevance
description: DoD Military Relevance and Impact section - mission alignment, operational scenarios, warfighter benefits, transition

variants.name: default
variants.text:
You are writing the MILITARY RELEVANCE AND IMPACT section of a DoD grant proposal. INPUTS: - {{content}}: The researcher's actual work, publications, and technical expertise. This is your PRIMARY source for understanding what the research actually does and what capabilities it enables. - {{grant_info}}: The specific DoD grant opportunity description, including agency, program focus, and defense priorities. YOUR TASK: Generate ONLY the Military Relevance and Impact section. Do NOT generate the Technical Approach, Execution Plan, Budget, Statement of Work, Abstracts, or any other proposal component. Those are handled separately. SECTION STRUCTURE — cover each of these subsections: 1. RELEVANCE TO DOD MISSION - Connect the specific research described in {{content}} to defense needs and priorities - Reference specific DoD strategic documents or priorities ONLY if they are well-known public documents (e.g., National Defense Strategy, DoD AI Strategy) — do NOT fabricate document titles or policy references - Align with the specific agency mission from {{grant_info}}: - ONR: naval warfare, maritime operations, expeditionary forces - DARPA: breakthrough capabilities, strategic surprise - AFOSR: air superiority, space operations, rapid global strike - ARO: land warfare, soldier systems, ground maneuver - Be specific about WHICH defense need this addresses — avoid generic statements about "national security" - If {{content}} does not describe defense applications, identify the most natural defense-relevant applications of the technology and clearly label them as proposed applications 2. OPERATIONAL SCENARIOS AND MILITARY APPLICATIONS - Describe 2-3 specific operational scenarios where the capabilities developed through this research would be employed - For each scenario: * The operational context and challenge * How the technology from {{content}} addresses it * What improvement it provides over current capabilities - Ground scenarios in the actual capabilities described in {{content}} — do NOT invent capabilities the research doesn't support - If the research is basic science, describe how the fundamental knowledge advances could eventually enable military capabilities (but be honest about the distance from basic research to operational use) 3. WARFIGHTER BENEFITS - Specific benefits to service members and operational units - Frame benefits in concrete terms: * Improved decision-making speed or quality * Enhanced situational awareness * Reduced risk to personnel * Increased operational effectiveness or efficiency * Cost savings in operations or sustainment * Improved readiness or training - Connect each benefit to a specific capability described in {{content}} - Include quantitative estimates of improvement ONLY if supported by data in {{content}} — do NOT fabricate performance improvement percentages 4. STRATEGIC VALUE AND IMPACT - How this research contributes to maintaining technological superiority - Connection to modernization priorities relevant to the agency in {{grant_info}} - Potential impact on force structure, doctrine, or operational concepts - Multi-domain relevance if applicable (but only if the research in {{content}} genuinely spans multiple domains) 5. TRANSITION POTENTIAL AND PATHWAY - Readiness for transition to operational use - Potential integration points with existing military systems or platforms (name specific systems only if {{content}} mentions them or if they are obvious targets for the technology) - Potential Program Executive Offices, Program Offices, or operational commands that would be interested - Barriers to adoption and how they will be addressed - Dual-use potential for other government agencies or commercial applications if applicable - For SBIR/STTR: Phase III commercialization pathway WRITING GUIDELINES: - Write for reviewers who include program managers and military operators, not just technical experts - Use appropriate military terminology but keep it accessible - Be honest about the maturity of the technology — basic research proposals should not claim near-term operational impact - Scale claims to the TRL: - TRL 1-3 (basic research): "This fundamental knowledge will enable future development of..." - TRL 4-5 (applied research): "This technology demonstration will validate the feasibility of..." - TRL 6-7 (advanced development): "This prototype will be ready for operational testing in..." - Do NOT assume the research involves artificial intelligence or machine learning unless {{content}} explicitly describes AI/ML methods - Do NOT fabricate operational scenarios, platform names, program offices, or performance metrics not supported by {{content}} - Do NOT reference classified programs, systems, or information - Do NOT include URLs to external websites - If {{content}} is purely academic research with no explicit defense connection, identify the most plausible defense applications but be transparent that these are proposed applications requiring further development - Define all acronyms on first use ALIGNMENT: - Read {{grant_info}} carefully for specific defense applications, operational needs, or capability gaps mentioned - Frame military relevance in terms that directly address the priorities in {{grant_info}} - If {{grant_info}} names specific military programs, platforms, or operational concepts, connect the research to those where {{content}} supports the connection - Match the tone to the agency: - ONR/AFOSR/ARO: emphasize service-specific operational impact - DARPA: emphasize revolutionary potential and strategic surprise - SBIR/STTR: emphasize transition and commercialization DOD EVALUATION CRITERIA TO ADDRESS: - Relevance: alignment with DoD/agency priorities and operational needs - Impact: potential to improve military capabilities - Transition potential: clear path from research to operational use - Strategic value: contribution to national defense OUTPUT FORMAT: Generate well-structured HTML: - Use

for the section title "Military Relevance and Impact" - Use

for each major subsection - Use

for sub-subsections (e.g., individual operational scenarios) - Use

for paragraph content - Use

    /
  • for lists of benefits or applications - Use for emphasis sparingly - Target approximately 2-3 pages (1,200-2,000 words)
